project: Federated Learning for Aircraft Engine PHM

00_eda.interpretation: C-MAPSS is structurally clean: 4 subsets, 709 training engines, ~160k rows, zero NaNs. FD001/FD003 are single-regime; FD002/FD004 mix 6 regimes. The literature 'drop 7 constant sensors' rule for FD001/FD003 matches 6 of 7 globally (sensor 6 is near-constant). The same rule for FD002/FD004 cannot be globally validated because regime variation dominates the global std — per-regime validation is deferred to Phase 2+. Engine lifetimes span 128–543 cycles; the 30-cycle sliding window is comfortably safe. Global fault positive rate (RUL ≤ 30) is uniform at 12.5–15.0% across subsets; per-client heterogeneity is engineered in P1 / P6 / RQ2.

01_data.interpretation: Stratified-by-lifetime partitioning of FD001 across 4 clients yields a deliberately balanced split: per-client fault positive rate 17.43–17.56% (spread 0.13 pp). This isolates 'does FedAvg converge?' from 'does FedAvg handle Non-IID?'. Meaningful Non-IID arrives in P6 (FD001+FD003 mix) and the RQ2 experiment.

02_smoke.interpretation: End-to-end smoke run on FD001: 1 epoch, 1.4s wall-clock. Loss decreased monotonically (766 → 530). Test AUPRC=0.845 after 1 epoch confirms the encoder is learning real degradation signal; the high RMSE/NASA values are the expected untrained-baseline behaviour and will close in the P3 50-epoch centralized run.

03_centralized.interpretation: Centralized upper-bound baseline on FD001: 50 epochs of cosine-annealed Adam (lr=0.001, wd=0.0001). Best epoch 5/50 reached RMSE=14.02, NASA=357, AUPRC=0.987. Total training time 85.3s on CPU. This is the upper-bound score every federated learning run (P4 local-only, P5 FedAvg) will be compared against.

04_local_only.interpretation: Lower-bound baseline on FD001: 4 clients trained in isolation, no weight sharing, same 50-epoch recipe as the P3 centralized run. Average per-client best RMSE = 15.02 ± 0.29  (range 14.76–15.50). Penalty for isolation vs centralized (P3 RMSE=14.02): +0.99 RMSE. The FedAvg run (P5) must close this gap to justify the federation.

05_fedavg.interpretation: FedAvg baseline on FD001: 4 clients × 50 communication rounds × 2 local epochs (400 total local-epoch equivalents). Best round 11/50 reached RMSE=14.16, NASA=350, AUPRC=0.965. Closed 85.9% of the local-only (15.02) → centralized (14.02) RMSE gap by sharing only model weights, never raw sensor data.
